title: Fluorescently labeled polyamine uptake (via Flow Cytometry)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"b42hb","text":"Assess polyamine uptake capacities of a specific cell line after incubation with fluorescently labeled polyamines and mean fluorescence intensitiy acquisition via flow cytometry. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":159,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"e7dec","text":"Make sure to stay protect from the light when manipulating fluorescent materials.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"aekr9","text":"Prepare appropriate dilution of fluorescently labeled polyamine in cell culture medium.\n","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"h2nh","text":"Prepare Flow Cytometry (FC) buffer made of 1% Albumin Fraction V in DPBS (-/-).\n","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6q7mt","text":"Prepare Eppendorf tubes and FC tubes by labeling them and keeping them on ice. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"7t31g","text":"0.25% Trypsin-EDTA: Gibco, 25200056","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ctdqn","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"acg6c","text":"Albumin Fraction V: Carl Roth, 8076.4","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e12cv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fb9fl","text":"Dulbecco's Phosphate Buffered Saline modified without calcium chloride and magnesium chloride (DPBS (-/-)): Gibco, D8537\n","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"crds","text":"TrypLE Express Enzyme: Gibco, 12604021\n","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bs5f7","text":"Versene Solution: Gibco, 15040","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: The day of the experiment, remove cell culture medium and add fluorescently labeled polyamines to the cells in a final volume of 500 µL, / well . Concentration and incubation time may vary depending on the cell type. It's best to try different incubation times and doses to better appreciate the kinetics. Stay protect from the light.
Incubate the desired time at 37°C, 5% CO 2 , protected from the light.
Wash with 500 µL, /well Versene or DPBS (-/-).
Add 500 µL, /well 1%-Albumin Fraction V in DPBS (-/-), collect the cells and transfer in Eppendorf tubes on ice.
Discard supernatants and resuspend cell pellets in 250-500 µL 1%-Albumin Fraction V in DPBS (-/-), depending on the size of the cell pellet.
Keep on ice until acquisition at the flow cytometer. Record 10,000 live events per sample.
Add 200 µL, /well 0.25% Trypsin or TrypLE. Incubate 00:05:00, at RT protect from the light.
Seed cells in 12 well-plate that they reach 70%-80% confluency the day of the experiment. This protocol is made for a 12 well-plate format. Adaptations need to be done to scale up or down.
Harvest cells and prepare samples for Flow Cytometry (FC).
Filter cell suspension through Nylon filter into FC tubes.
creator: Marine Houdou
